items.caption:
Bad news for my nafs: Allah loves the version of me it told me to hide. 🤍

You know that part of you you've been tucking away? The one that cries too easily, falls too often, still hasn't figured it out? Your nafs convinced you that part needed to be fixed before you could be loved. So you performed. You pushed. You showed up to prayer exhausted and felt nothing — and called that your fault too.

But here is the thing the nafs never wanted you to know: Allah's love did not begin after your healing. It was there before your becoming. It is the reason you are still here.

No human love reaches the depth of your soul. No human love stays when they see everything. His does. His always has.

Save this for the night your nafs starts reading that list out loud again.

Ready to come home to that love? Comment HEAL and I'll send you a free soul reflection to begin. 🤍

#IslamicReminder #MuslimWomen #Divinelove #TrustAllah #IslamicHealing #SoulfullMuslimaah #BecomingHer

Carrying that guilt didn't make me closer to Allah. It kept me away. 🤍

There's a version of tawbah we were never taught — the one where you return not because you've suffered enough, but because He is already waiting. We confused guilt with sincerity. We thought staying in the shame was part of paying the price. It isn't. It is just the nafs wearing piety as a mask.

His rahmah is not a reward for your perfect return. It is the ground you return to.

Swipe through — one truth per slide. Let it settle before you move on.

Save this and send it to the friend who needs to know she hasn't fallen too far.

Ready to begin your return? Comment HEAL and I'll send you a free soul reflection. 🤍

#IslamicReminder #MuslimWomen #Tawbah #AllahsMercy #IslamicHealing #SoulfullMuslimaah #BecomingHer #Rahmah

POV: It's 2am and you're still trying to solve the thing you already gave to Allah. 🤍

Everyone else is asleep. The house is quiet. And the thought you outran all day has finally caught you — the one about how it could go wrong, the one that runs the scenarios, the one that whispers *what if you should have done something different by now*. You called it tawakkul. You said the words. You meant them. But here you are, holding it again, turning it over in the dark.

This is not a failure of faith. This is the nafs doing what it was made to do. But tawakkul is not a one-time handover — it is the practice of putting it down every time you pick it up again.

Allah does not need you to control this outcome. He already holds it.

Comment a 🤍 if you're in this season right now.

Share this with the sister who's been lying awake doing the same thing.

If you're ready to stop carrying what was never yours: Comment HEAL and I'll send you a free soul reflection to begin. 🤍

#Tawakkul #TrustAllah #MuslimWomen #IslamicReminder #MuslimWomenHealing #SoulfullMuslimaah #BecomingHer

You weren't broken. You were buried. And buried things still grow. 🤍

You know that feeling — like you've drifted so far from who you used to be that you can't remember the way back. Like the version of you who once felt close to Allah belongs to someone else's story. You called it moving on. You called it being realistic. But part of you never stopped aching for her.

Here's what I need you to hear: that ache *is* your fitrah. The part of you that Allah placed inside you before the world got loud. It doesn't die. It doesn't leave. It waits.

Your return isn't a reward you have to earn. It's a door He never locked.

Make your life 'alhamdulillah I came back' — not 'I wish I'd returned sooner.'

Send this to the woman who thinks she's too far gone to come back — she needs to see this today.

If you're ready to find your way back to her — the her you were always meant to become — Comment HER to join the Becoming Her waitlist. 🤍

#MuslimWomen #IslamicReminders #TrustAllah #MuslimSelfCare #IslamicWellness #SoulfullMuslimaah #BecomingHer

3 signs your overthinking is fear — not faith. (Swipe before you spiral again.) 🤍

You've been calling it being thorough. Being responsible. Maybe even being wise. But somewhere between the third scenario you've run in your head and the sleep you didn't get, something feels off — and it's not your planning. It's the knot that never loosens no matter how much you think.

Tawakkul was never meant to be the thing you reach for after you've exhausted every other option. It's the ground you stand on before you take a single step.

Swipe through — each slide is one sign to sit with slowly.

Save this for the next 2am spiral — you'll want these words when your mind won't quiet down.

If this landed somewhere tender, your healing doesn't have to wait. Comment HEAL and I'll send you a free soul reflection to begin. 🤍

#MuslimWomen #IslamicReminders #Tawakkul #MuslimMentalHealth #IslamicWellness #SoulfullMuslimaah #BecomingHer #TrustAllah

items.theme: People-pleasing — He never asked you to empty yourself
items.caption:
Unfortunately, if you spend long enough here, I'll convince you that you were never selfish — only starving. 🤍

You learned early that making yourself smaller made the room feel safer. That saying yes kept the peace. That your needs were the inconvenient ones — the ones that could wait, the ones that didn't really count. So you poured and poured, and when the cup ran dry you blamed yourself for running out.

But He never asked for your depletion. He asked for your sincerity.

There is no ayah that says your worth is measured in how much of yourself you gave away. Rahmah — mercy — begins with the self He entrusted to you. You cannot pour from a cup you were never allowed to fill.

Self-abandonment was never obedience. It was a wound dressed up as virtue.

Save this for the moment someone makes you feel guilty for having a limit — because that moment will come again.

Comment 🤍 if you needed to read this tonight.

When you're ready to stop running on empty and start coming home to yourself — Comment HEAL and I'll send you a free soul reflection to begin.

#MuslimWomen #IslamicReminders #MuslimMentalHealth #IslamicSelfCare #IslamicWellness #SoulfullMuslimaah #BecomingHer #HealingInIslam

If love made you walk on eggshells, you were never loved — you were managed.

You learned to read the air before you spoke. You learned to shrink yourself down to a shape that felt safe. You called that love because no one ever showed you the difference — and for a long time, neither did I.

Here is the one thing I needed someone to tell me: rahmah — the mercy Allah built into real love — never asks you to disappear inside it. It is the root of the word womb. It is warm, not watchful. It holds, it does not audit.

When you finally meet love that does not cost you your peace to keep, you will know. And until then, Allah's rahmah is the template. Rest in that. 🤍

Save this for the moment you catch yourself apologising for simply existing in a room.

If this is stirring something in you, there is a free soul reflection waiting for you.
Comment HEAL and I'll send you a free soul reflection to begin.

#MuslimWomen #IslamicReminder #RahmahIsReal #MuslimMentalHealth #HealingInIslam #BecomingHer #SoulfullMuslimaah
